behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both files (packages/excalidraw/clipboard.ts, packages/excalidraw/components/FilledButton.scss), the function (copyTextToSystemClipboard), the variable (plainTextEntry), the SCSS selectors, and the required CSS property background-color: var(--color-success).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the three-tier strategy and code quality issues but leaves the agent to determine the actual code structure changes (return placement, etc.).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex patterns flexibly matching the required behavior (return inside if block, return after writeText, const declaration, absence of undefined assignment) rather than exact line text.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All behavior tests in test_outputs.py use regex on file contents (re.search on CLIPBOARD_TS.read_text() and BUTTON_SCSS.read_text()); no test actually imports/runs the clipboard function. Pass-to-pass tests only run prettier syntax checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each assertion checks a specific structural requirement (return placement, const keyword, absence of undefined assignment, background-color property) that a stub couldn't satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the three required changes in clipboard.ts (return placement, const, no undefined) and the SCSS background-color addition.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively (test_clipboard_flow_control, test_plain_text_const, etc.) and grouped with a pass-to-pass section separator comment.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code-fix, not structured data.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers match between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies files but doesn't give exact line numbers or the exact diff; the file scope is inherent to the task.
